title: commit,build: --source-date-epoch/--timestamp omit identity label
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind bug

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

When using either --source-date-epoch or --timestamp, default to not adding a label with our version number in it, since it can change between builds.

#### How to verify it

New integration test!

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
`buildah build` no longer sets/updates the `io.buildah.version` label by default when either `--timestamp` or `--source-date-epoch` are used.
```

comments.body:
LGTM
Looks like the branch need some updating @nalind

> Following PR assumes that if both flags are used the use-case is `reproduce-able` builds and it removes the identity label and documentation doesn't say so. Maybe docs should say why is it removing identify label for such use-case.

I don't think I follow — if `--identity-label` is set to `false`, the label is neither set nor cleared, so if the base image had a value set for it, it'll be carried over.  I was tempted to have it clear the value for `--identity-label=false` cases when implementing this, but that would have been a bigger change from the previous behavior, so I limited the scope to adding the condition to the not-specified-either-way case.  Adding a test that confirms that.
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
